title: alectra parasitica subsp. parasitica
intro: Alectra parasitica subsp. parasitica, also known as the Dodder, is a parasitic flowering plant that grows by twining around its host plant, obtaining nutrients through the host's vascular tissue. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its medicinal properties are attributed to its alkaloid and glycoside content. The health benefits associated with Alectra parasitica subsp. parasitica include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may be useful in treating various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. In traditional herbal preparations, Alectra parasitica subsp. parasitica is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and ointments, although the effectiveness and safety of these preparations require further scientific study.
